payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.title: President, Metro-North Railroad / Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: Cathy Rinaldi President, Metro-North Railroad Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.title: Chief Mechanical Officer, Long Island Rail Road
payload.description: Previously Master Mechanic, then General Manager-Fleet Support, now Chief Mechanical Officer for LIRR. Former assembler and field supervisor for Bombardier.

evidence_refs.source_quote: I'd like to introduce our brand-new Chief Mechanical Officer for the Long Island Rail Road, Brad Jenkins.
As an assembler and field supervisor for Bombardier in a former life, Brad knows rolling stock and every piece therein

payload.description: Works with LIRR and Metro-North on major infrastructure projects.

evidence_refs.source_quote: LIRR and MTA Construction & Development have already completed necessary repairs to the parking garage
Metro-North and MTA Construction & Development are awarding a design-build contract to replace the decades-old Pelham Substation on the New Haven Line.
The number of criminal summonses issued for fare evasion, the number of criminal summonses issued for other infractions, and the number of arrests made by MTA Police Department
We cooperated with the Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) independent review of this incident

tool_args.label: Rehabilitation of Ronkonkoma Station Parking Garage

payload.project_name: Rehabilitation of Ronkonkoma Station Parking Garage
payload.description: Repairs at the Ronkonkoma Station parking garage including repainting steel beams, structural repairs, replacing staircases, preparing surfaces, painting steel components of garage and adjoining pedestrian overpass, new fire alarm, fire standpipe system, and new doors throughout the garage. Pedestrian overpass improvement work continuing.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Rehabilitation of Ronkonkoma Station Parking Garage Underway
Improving parking at LIRR stations is a priority under the MTA's 2020 – 2024 Capital Program

tool_args.label: New Haven Line Pelham Substation Replacement

payload.description: Replacing the decades-old Pelham Substation on the New Haven Line with a new walk-in substation connected to the third rail system. Includes electrical, communications, and circuitry equipment to improve power supply capacity and resiliency. New facility will be elevated on waterproof cable vault to withstand flooding. Design-build contract being awarded by Metro-North and MTA Construction & Development.
payload.agency: Metro-North Railroad and MTA Construction & Development

evidence_refs.source_quote: New Haven Line's Pelham Substation Gets an Upgrade

tool_args.label: New Canaan Branch Line Track Improvement

payload.project_name: New Canaan Branch Line Track Improvement
payload.description: Major track improvement project on the New Canaan Branch Line. Train service resumed on September 2 after summer work. Completion eliminated a significant speed restriction that impacted New Haven Line customers.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North also completed a major track improvement project on the New Canaan Branch Line. Train service resumed on September 2 after months of hard work during the summer heat waves, and its completion eliminated a significant speed restriction that impacted New Haven Line customers.

payload.description: Rail Safety Week across the US, Canada and Mexico. LIRR and Metro-North participating. Sponsored by Operation Lifesaver. Theme: 'Stop Track Tragedies'. payload.description: Derailment of a Hempstead-bound passenger train just east of Jamaica at Hall Interlocking. Caused 135 late trains over a two-day period, delayed customers an average of 9 minutes. Caused by wiring issue in a single switch. No severe injuries. 55 customers onboard, all 8 cars rerailed by following afternoon.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the derailment of a Hempstead-bound passenger train on August 3
